sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 46-year-old male, presented to the office with a complaint of right knee pain after a trampoline incident one week ago.
He reported hearing a snap and experiencing immediate pain.
He has not sought medical attention until now, despite experiencing persistent pain.
He has not tried any treatments for the pain except for Tylenol, which provided minimal relief.
He has a history of hypertension and diabetes, for which he is currently taking lisinopril 20mg and metformin 500mg daily.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, for which he is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily.
He also has diabetes, for which he is taking metformin 500mg daily.
He has not measured his blood pressure regularly, despite my previous recommendation to do so.
Review of systems: The patient denied any other symptoms.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily, metformin 500mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 180/80, pulse rate [NOT MENTIONED], oxygen saturation [NOT MENTIONED], temperature [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: The patient had a regular heart rate and rhythm, clear lungs bilaterally, and a slight limp when walking.
There was ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of the knee, but no effusion.
The patient had a decent range of motion, but pain with movement.
Test Results: Right knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee and hypertension and diabetes.
Problem list: Lateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Diabetes; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling related to the lateral ligament strain.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I continued the patient's current medications, including lisinopril 20mg daily.
I ordered an echo to assess the patient's cardiac function and blood pressure control.
I also ordered an A1c and lipid panel to assess the patient's diabetes management.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the ordered tests and to reassess the patient's knee and diabetes management.
